answer:
The verse is found in Yājñavalkya Smṛti chapter 1, verses Yj_1.4–1.5 (work slug: unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6). The two-verse passage reads:

"Manu, Atri, Viṣṇu, Hārīta, Yājñavalkya, Uśanas, Aṅgiras,
Yama, Āpastamba, Saṃvarta, Kātyāyana, and Bṛhaspati, // Yj_1.4 //

Parāśara, Vyāsa, Śaṅkha, Likhita, Dakṣa, and Gautama,
Śātātapa, and Vasiṣṭha are the promulgators of the Dharmaśāstras. // Yj_1.5 //"

The twenty promulgators named are: Manu, Atri, Viṣṇu, Hārīta, Yājñavalkya, Uśanas, Aṅgiras, Yama, Āpastamba, Saṃvarta, Kātyāyana, Bṛhaspati, Parāśara, Vyāsa, Śaṅkha, Likhita, Dakṣa, Gautama, Śātātapa, and Vasiṣṭha (ending with Vasiṣṭha as specified).
